contracts.businessImpact: Visitors cannot inspect the location or interact with the map.
contracts.risk: A full-card overlay can block pointer and touch interaction with the embedded map.
assertions: The page contains exactly one .map-live-frame iframe.
The iframe URL is an HTTPS Google Maps embed with output=embed.
The iframe remains keyboard reachable and must not use tabindex=-1.
The iframe receives pointer events.
The full-card .map-live-link overlay does not receive pointer events.
The visible Google Maps badge and route panel remain clickable.

contracts.businessImpact: The first screen loses motion and looks like a broken static poster.
contracts.risk: Calling video.load() without an explicit playback recovery can leave the hero on a static poster in mobile Chrome.
assertions: The hero contains exactly one muted, looping, inline autoplay video.
The video source is a local MP4 asset.
The runtime explicitly calls video.play().
Playback is retried on canplay, page visibility restoration and first pointer interaction.
Unexpected pauses trigger playback recovery while the page is visible.

contracts.businessImpact: The landing appears visually broken or uniformly black.
contracts.risk: An overly opaque hero overlay or missing light-section backgrounds can make the entire landing appear black.
assertions: The hero overlay maximum alpha does not exceed 0.78.
The hero video has a brightness filter of at least 1.0.
About and visit sections retain the paper background.
The gallery retains the cream background.
Only designated contrast sections use the dark palette.

contracts.area: Navigation, deep links and critical CTAs
contracts.businessImpact: Visitors cannot reach the menu, a category, Instagram or directions.
contracts.risk: A renamed file, invalid hash or malformed relative URL can silently break the primary customer journey on GitHub Pages.
assertions: Every internal anchor points to an existing local file.
Every hash points to a static section or a real menu category.
The home page contains exactly six unique menu preview deep links.
Every preview deep link targets a category defined in menu-data.js.
The route CTA targets Roby's Coffee House Gazipaşa.
The Instagram CTA targets @robyscoffeehouse.

contracts.businessImpact: Customers see missing products, duplicated products or incorrect prices.
contracts.risk: Menu data is business content and can regress independently of the page layout.
assertions: Category ids are unique and URL-safe.
Every category and group contains at least one item.
Every category, group, item and description is translated into TR, EN and RU.
Every price is a positive integer TRY amount.
Item names are not duplicated inside the same category.
Lotus Cheesecake remains protected at 190 ₺.
Displayed prices use Turkish formatting and the ₺ symbol.

contracts.businessImpact: A visitor sees mixed languages or loses their selected language between pages.
contracts.risk: The main page and menu use different translation mechanisms and can drift apart.
assertions: Both pages expose exactly TR, EN and RU language buttons.
All menuCopy languages have identical non-empty keys.
All menu data translations are complete.
Both runtimes share the robys-language storage key.
Both runtimes update document.documentElement.lang.
Language persistence fails safely when localStorage is unavailable.
The clear-search control has localized labels in all three languages.

contracts.area: Menu search, category filtering and clearing
contracts.businessImpact: Customers cannot find products in the full menu.
contracts.risk: Search can fail through normalization, language, category-filter or clear-button changes.
assertions: Search normalizes user input and filters item names and descriptions.
Search works against TR, EN and RU menu values.
Category and text filters work together.
A no-results state appears for unmatched queries.
The clear button and Escape use the standard input event flow.
Focus returns to the search field after clearing.
Searchable content is rendered with textContent rather than innerHTML.

contracts.area: Responsive layout and fixed mobile controls
contracts.businessImpact: Phone users see clipped content, horizontal overflow or controls covering the page.
contracts.risk: Small CSS changes can remove safe-area spacing, collapse breakpoints or reintroduce the empty third mobile CTA column.
assertions: Both pages keep width=device-width and viewport-fit=cover.
The main layout retains 980px, 680px and 390px breakpoints.
The fixed CTA keeps two equal columns and bottom safe-area support.
The page reserves bottom space for the fixed CTA.
Critical mobile tap targets stay at least 46px tall.
Menu categories remain horizontally scrollable.
The full menu collapses to one column with stable product and price columns.

contracts.area: Directions, Instagram and visible business details
contracts.businessImpact: Visitors cannot navigate to the café or reach its social profile.
contracts.risk: Duplicated CTA markup can drift to inconsistent destinations or unsafe external-link settings.
assertions: External links use noopener and noreferrer.
Every directions CTA uses one consistent Roby's Gazipaşa destination.
Every Instagram CTA targets @robyscoffeehouse.
The street and city address remain visible on the landing.
Opening hours remain visible in key landing sections.
The full menu keeps a localized directions CTA.

contracts.area: Keyboard navigation, labels and semantic state
contracts.businessImpact: Keyboard and assistive-technology users cannot navigate or understand the interface.
contracts.risk: Visual refactors can silently remove labels, focus indication, live regions or modal focus containment.
assertions: Both pages declare language and working skip links.
Buttons explicitly use type=button.
Images expose alt attributes and decorative media stays hidden.
The mobile menu toggle exposes controls and expanded state.
The search field keeps an explicit label and polite live results.
The map iframe keeps a descriptive title.
Global focus-visible styling remains present.
Lightbox focus trapping and inert background protection remain active.
Dynamic category state remains exposed through aria-pressed.

contracts.area: Local media, icons and runtime asset references
contracts.businessImpact: The landing ships broken images, missing styles, empty icons or an invalid hero video.
contracts.risk: Renaming or deleting a local file can break the deployed static site without a compile-time import error.
assertions: Every local HTML href, src and poster reference resolves to a non-empty file.
Every local CSS url() reference resolves to a non-empty file.
The canonical poster and H.264 hero MP4 remain present.
All six menu icon files remain present and uniquely mapped.
The image fallback remains connected to the canonical poster.
The canonical MP4 codec and size verifier remains wired through verify:media.

contracts.area: Search metadata, social cards and structured data
contracts.businessImpact: Search engines and social previews show missing, duplicated or incorrect page information.
contracts.risk: Metadata duplicated across static pages can drift independently from canonical URLs and structured data.
assertions: Each public page has one bounded title and description.
Robots directives remain indexable with large image previews.
Canonical and Open Graph URLs match the public project URLs.
Open Graph site name, locale, image and image alt remain complete.
Twitter large-image cards remain complete.
Each page exposes exactly one parseable Schema.org JSON-LD block.
The landing uses CafeOrCoffeeShop structured data.
The menu uses Menu structured data and links back to the café entity.

contracts.area: Canonical café identity, address, hours and destinations
contracts.businessImpact: Visitors receive incorrect location, opening-hour or contact information.
contracts.risk: Repeated business details can drift between visible content, JSON-LD, menu CTAs and social links.
assertions: A versioned business profile is the canonical comparison source.
Visible name, street, locality and region match the profile.
Visible opening hours remain present in all key landing locations.
Structured address and opening hours match the profile.
Structured landing, menu and image URLs match the profile.
Instagram links and handle match @robyscoffeehouse.
Map links consistently target Roby's Coffee House Gazipaşa.

contracts.area: GitHub Pages artifact and live publication
contracts.businessImpact: The repository passes code checks but publishes missing, stale or project-path-broken pages.
contracts.risk: Project Pages can break through root-relative URLs, missing root files, stale builds or delayed publication.
assertions: index.html, menu.html, 404.html, robots.txt, sitemap.xml and .nojekyll remain present.
Landing and menu expose the same build marker.
Public pages contain no localhost, file or project-unsafe root-relative URLs.
robots.txt exposes the canonical sitemap.
sitemap.xml lists the landing and menu canonical URLs.
The 404 page remains noindex and returns visitors to the project root.
The retrying live verifier confirms both pages, robots and sitemap after merge.

contracts.businessImpact: A technically valid change can still ship shifted layouts, overlapping prices, clipped content or broken mobile controls.
contracts.risk: Static code assertions cannot detect visible movement, wrapping, overlap, spacing or composition regressions.
assertions: The landing and full menu are captured at 320, 390, 768 and 1440 pixel widths.
Hero, menu preview, visit/map and mobile CTA receive dedicated element screenshots.
Every pull request is compared with its exact base commit in the same Chromium environment.
Animations, transitions, video frames and external map pixels are stabilized before capture.
Pixel differences use bounded per-capture tolerances no greater than one percent.
Any comparison above tolerance blocks the pull request.
Baseline, current and diff screenshots are uploaded as CI evidence even on failure.

contracts.area: Core Web Vitals and static asset budgets
contracts.businessImpact: The site becomes slow on mobile, delays menu discovery or consumes excessive data.
contracts.risk: New media, scripts or styling can silently degrade user-perceived speed even when functionality remains correct.
assertions: Lighthouse audits both the landing and full menu with three runs per device profile.
Mobile performance remains at least 0.85.
Mobile LCP stays at or below 2500 ms, TBT at or below 200 ms and CLS at or below 0.1.
Desktop performance remains at least 0.95.
Desktop LCP stays at or below 1500 ms, TBT at or below 100 ms and CLS at or below 0.05.
Total referenced JavaScript and the largest JavaScript file stay within byte budgets.
Referenced CSS, HTML, hero video and poster stay within byte budgets.
Any Core Web Vitals or asset-budget breach blocks the pull request.

contracts.area: Published customer journeys in a real browser
contracts.businessImpact: The repository is green while the public site is stale, broken or unusable for customers.
contracts.risk: GitHub Pages publication, caching, browser runtime or asset delivery can fail after repository checks have passed.
assertions: The smoke runs after every push to main and on a daily schedule.
Landing and menu must publish the expected build marker before the smoke proceeds.
Landing, menu, robots, sitemap, hero video and poster return successful HTTP responses.
A real 390px Chromium session opens the public landing without same-origin console or page errors.
The hero video successfully starts playback.
The embedded map source and two-link mobile CTA remain present.
The full menu renders at least twenty products.
Searching for Lotus returns a result and Escape restores the menu.
Switching to English updates and persists the document language.
A JSON evidence report is uploaded even when the live smoke fails.

contracts.area: DOM XSS and unsafe browser sinks
contracts.businessImpact: A malicious string executes code in a visitor browser or rewrites trusted content.
contracts.risk: Translations, search text, URL state or future CMS data could reach an executable DOM sink.
assertions: Runtime code contains no innerHTML or outerHTML assignments.
insertAdjacentHTML, document.write, eval, new Function and string timers are forbidden.
Rich translations construct only text nodes, br and em elements.
javascript URLs and inline event handlers are forbidden.
Every target=_blank link keeps noopener and noreferrer.
The Google Maps iframe stays inside the explicit origin allowlist.

contracts.businessImpact: Compromised markup can load arbitrary scripts, frames, objects or outbound connections.
contracts.risk: A weak or missing CSP removes an important containment layer for XSS and supply-chain failures.
assertions: Landing and menu expose the same strict meta CSP.
Scripts and styles load only from self without unsafe-inline or unsafe-eval.
Frames are limited to maps.google.com.
Objects, forms and workers are disabled.
Trusted Types are required for script sinks and no policies may be created.
Executable inline scripts and inline style attributes are forbidden.
Both pages keep strict-origin-when-cross-origin referrer policy.

contracts.businessImpact: A compromised build dependency can alter the published site or CI output.
contracts.risk: Development dependencies execute during trusted builds even when the browser has no runtime packages.
assertions: CI installs dependencies with npm ci.
High and critical npm advisories block the security workflow.
package-lock.json remains review-protected.
Dependabot monitors npm dependencies weekly.
Dependabot monitors GitHub Actions weekly.
Security evidence is retained as a CI artifact.

contracts.businessImpact: A leaked token can compromise repositories, services or future production infrastructure.
contracts.risk: Secrets can enter through source, documentation, configuration or copied debugging output.
assertions: The current text tree is scanned for common provider tokens and private keys.
Added lines since the PR base or previous push are scanned.
Findings are redacted in logs and reports.
Placeholder values are ignored without weakening provider-specific patterns.
Any credible finding blocks the workflow.
The report is uploaded even when scanning fails.

contracts.businessImpact: Unsafe data flow or injection patterns reach main without a dedicated security analysis.
contracts.risk: Pattern checks cannot model every cross-file data flow or newly introduced vulnerability class.
assertions: CodeQL analyzes JavaScript and TypeScript.
Security-extended queries are enabled.
Analysis runs on pull requests and main pushes.
Analysis also runs on a weekly schedule.
The workflow can publish security events.
Repository contents remain read-only to the analysis job.

contracts.businessImpact: An unreviewed workflow or security-control change bypasses the project safety gates.
contracts.risk: The build pipeline is a privileged attack surface and can be easier to compromise than the static site.
assertions: Security workflow contents permission is read-only.
CodeQL receives only the permissions required to publish findings.
Security-sensitive paths are assigned to the maintainer through CODEOWNERS.
Workflow, lockfile, security scripts and dashboard changes require review.
Security checks run on pull requests and main.
Security and threat-response documentation remain versioned in the repository.

contracts.businessImpact: A future feature silently expands visitor tracking or persists sensitive behavior.
contracts.risk: Analytics or personalization can gradually become data collection without an explicit product decision.
assertions: The only approved localStorage key is robys-language.
Menu search terms are not transmitted by the current runtime.
The current analytics buffer remains in memory and dataLayer only.
No browser secret or credential is required.
Any new storage key must update the privacy contract.
Any new external connection must update the CSP allowlist and threat model.

contracts.businessImpact: A browser trust boundary accepts attacker-shaped data even though static checks appear green.
contracts.risk: DOM behavior, CSP enforcement and persisted input can differ from source-level assumptions in a real browser.
assertions: Chromium runs with the production CSP enabled.
Trusted Types blocks direct markup assignment.
Inline and unapproved external scripts remain inert.
Stored language values, menu search text and URL fragments remain non-executable.
Safe rich translations contain only BR and EM descendants.
Page-load requests stay within the explicit origin allowlist.
Browser storage remains limited to robys-language.
Structured evidence is retained on success and failure.

contracts.businessImpact: A browser-visible security weakness reaches the reviewed build without dynamic inspection.
contracts.risk: Static analysis cannot observe every response, link, browser policy or dynamically reachable page.
assertions: ZAP scans the locally built static site.
The spider and passive scanner inspect reachable pages and assets.
Warnings remain visible in retained reports.
Confirmed scanner failures block the pull request.
The workflow cannot create public issues automatically.
Repository permissions remain read-only.
The scan report is retained as CI evidence.

contracts.businessImpact: The public GitHub Pages artifact differs from the reviewed repository without detection.
contracts.risk: Publication, caching or unauthorized modification can serve bytes that do not match the approved source tree.
assertions: A deterministic SHA-256 manifest inventories public files.
The landing and menu entry pages are included.
Local byte lengths and digests are verified before merge.
Manifest, landing and menu build markers must agree.
After publication every manifest entry is downloaded and hashed.
Any missing file, size mismatch or digest mismatch blocks verification.
Live verification emits a retained JSON evidence report.
